title: Refine Metrics API Response Format, Versioning, and Public IDs
description:
## Context

After initial testing of the Metrics API, several refinements were identified to improve the developer experience, security, and response format before the API is consumed by external integrators.

## Changes

### 1. Public-Facing Metric IDs
Replace internal database IDs with longer, externally-referenceable public IDs. Internal IDs must not be exposed to customers or developers. Existing metrics need to be backfilled with public IDs.

### 2. API Versioning
Implement URI-based versioning (e.g., `/api/v1_0/metrics`) so that existing API consumers are not broken when new versions are released. Each version is frozen once published.

### 3. Strip Unnecessary Fields
Remove internal-only data points from the customer-facing API response.

### 4. Top-Level Array Format
Return metrics as a top-level JSON array instead of wrapping in `{metrics: [...]}`.

### 5. Exclude Deleted Metrics
Filter out soft-deleted metrics (where `is_deleted` is true) from API responses.

### 6. Clean Response Shape
Standardize the response to include: `id` (public), `name`, `definition`, `business_owner`, `related_date_time`, `target_interval`, `created_at`, `updated_at`, and `aliases` (without the redundant `is_primary` field).

### 7. Line of Business and Topic Arrays
Include Line of Business and Topic label arrays in each metric response.

### 8. Single Metric GET Endpoint
Add `GET /api/v1_0/metrics/{public_id}` to retrieve a single metric by its public ID.
acceptance_criteria: Metrics are returned with public-facing IDs instead of internal database IDs
All existing metrics are backfilled with public IDs
Internal-only fields are stripped from the API response
Metrics response is a top-level JSON array
Soft-deleted metrics are excluded from API responses
Alias objects do not include the is_primary field
Each metric includes Line of Business and Topic label arrays
GET /api/v1_0/metrics/{public_id} returns a single metric by public ID
Requesting a non-existent or unauthorized metric ID returns an appropriate error (not an empty array)
middleware_changes: API response serialization updated to use public IDs, strip internal fields, flatten the response to a top-level array, remove is_primary from aliases, and include LOB/Topic labels. Deleted metrics are filtered from query results.
data_model_changes: New public-facing ID column added to the metrics table. Existing metrics backfilled with generated public IDs.
api_changes: GET /api/v1_0/metrics — updated response format with public IDs, clean field set, LOB/Topic arrays. GET /api/v1_0/metrics/{public_id} — new endpoint for single metric retrieval. URI-based versioning implemented across all API routes.
implementation_notes: URI-based versioning was implemented so endpoints follow the pattern /api/v1_0/metrics. All 8 refinement items were implemented and deployed to staging. Testing revealed that requesting an invalid public ID returned an empty array instead of an error — this was flagged for follow-up error handling work (see MBT-920).
comment_summary: Waqar implemented all 8 items sequentially on staging, confirming each as done. Ryan tested and identified two issues: requesting an invalid metric ID returned an empty array instead of an error, and some metrics lacked backfilled public IDs.
